title: onobrychis alba var. echinata
intro: Onobrychis alba var. echinata, also known as hairy sainfoin or sainfoin, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white flowers and hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Onobrychis alba var. echinata include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of Onobrychis alba var. echinata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally or used externally to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
